constant_power_discharge.label: Constant power discharge test
constant_power_discharge.intent: Characterize capacity and energy under constant-power discharge across a temperature and power matrix.
applications: Temperature dependence of usable energy content

steps.name: Define the power matrix
steps.details: Set constant-power discharge levels that remove approximately 75%, 50%, and 25% of rated energy in one hour, while respecting manufacturer current and voltage limits.

steps.name: Define the temperature matrix
steps.details: Use the recommended 5, 25, and 45 C set, and add sub-zero discharge points only when the application requires them and charge safety remains controlled.

steps.name: Standard-cycle precharge
steps.details: Charge the cell as described in the standard cycle no more than 48 h before the constant-power run.

steps.name: Rest and logging setup
steps.details: Rest for {rest_minutes} minutes and keep at least minute-level logging plus threshold-based capture for voltage, capacity, and temperature changes.

steps.name: Constant-power discharge
steps.details: Discharge at the selected power level until {discharge_cutoff_v} V, then rest for {rest_minutes} minutes.

steps.name: CC-CV recharge
steps.details: Recharge at the manufacturer nominal current to {charge_voltage_v} V, complete the CV taper to the slow-discharge cut-off current, and rest for {rest_minutes} minutes.

steps.name: Repeat replicates
steps.details: Repeat the discharge-rest-charge block three times for each power level, then repeat for each selected temperature after at least 2 h of thermal equilibration.

data_acquisition: Use minute-level logging plus event-based triggers for voltage, capacity, and temperature changes where the cycler supports them.
Track surface temperature closely at high power because the chapter explicitly warns about thermal limits.

graphs: Voltage trace vs time for each power rate
Temperature rise vs power level and temperature

strict_reference_policy.summary: Keep the standard-cycle precharge, 60 min rest framing, constant-power discharge, and three-repeat structure locked. Narrow the power or temperature matrix only after review.
tailorable_fields: exact power values within the chapter logic
extended relaxation criterion when EMF-style recovery matters

dynamic_stress_test.label: Dynamic stress test
dynamic_stress_test.intent: Evaluate automotive dynamic power-following capability with a scaled USABC-style DST profile.
applications: Dynamic discharge capability screening before life testing

steps.name: Define the DST power basis
steps.details: Choose the 100% DST scaling basis from the USABC goal, manufacturer ratings, or a prior peak-power test, then freeze that basis before execution.

steps.name: Charge and stabilize
steps.details: Bring the cell to full charge and stabilize temperature according to the approved test plan before the dynamic profile starts.

steps.name: Run the continuous DST profile
steps.details: Apply the 360 s DST profile continuously end-to-end with no rest between profiles and with at most 1 s transition time between power steps.

steps.name: Enforce endpoint logic
steps.details: Stop at rated capacity for performance or reference tests, or at 80% of rated capacity for baseline life-cycle use, or sooner if the cell can no longer follow the profile within limits.

steps.name: High-speed logging
steps.details: Use roughly 500 ms sampling so the automotive transients and profile-following quality remain visible.

data_acquisition: Use approximately 500 ms constant sample time for voltage, current, and power.
Track net capacity removed because the endpoint logic is based on Ah removed after any regenerative contribution.

graphs: Voltage and power trace over the DST profile

strict_reference_policy.summary: Keep the continuous 360 s DST execution, no-rest profile chaining, and endpoint logic locked. Only the scaling basis and application-specific endpoint choice move after review.

drive_cycle_test.label: Drive cycle test
drive_cycle_test.intent: Cycle the cell with a realistic NEDC or WLTP-style profile so life can be linked back to driven-distance style duty rather than a single constant C-rate.
applications: Vehicle-profile benchmarking before formal ageing campaigns

steps.name: Standard charge
steps.details: Charge at approximately C/3 to the end-of-charge voltage before each drive-cycle discharge block.

steps.name: Pre-profile pause
steps.details: Pause for {rest_minutes} minutes before starting the NEDC or WLTP profile.

steps.name: Run the drive profile
steps.details: Execute the selected NEDC or WLTP discharge profile until {discharge_cutoff_v} V or the approved end-of-discharge criterion.

steps.name: Repeat to end of life
steps.details: Repeat the pause-profile-charge sequence until the agreed end-of-life threshold is reached.

data_acquisition: Preserve time-resolved current, voltage, and temperature through the non-constant-rate profile.
Track equivalent distance or equivalent cycles only after the exact drive-cycle mapping is frozen.

graphs: Voltage trace across repeated drive cycles
Capacity or delivered energy vs repeated profile count

strict_reference_policy.summary: Keep the charge-pause-drive-profile-repeat structure locked. The chosen drive-cycle family and end-of-life translation still need review.
locked_planning_fields: 30 min pause before the drive profile
tailorable_fields: NEDC versus WLTP or derived profile choice
